title: Spotting Scams and Phishing
drDigitalIntro:
Phishing uses fake messages to steal your information.
• They pretend to be your bank, a delivery service, or a trusted company.
• Warning signs: urgency, misspelled addresses, and mismatched links.
• Hover over a link to see where it really goes — when in doubt, don't click.

playgroundTask.goal: You identified safe and dangerous links

steps.say: Click 'Reveal URL' on 'Verify your account' to see where the link really goes.
That URL goes to fakesite.ru — mark it as Dangerous.
Now reveal the URL for 'View your order'.
That URL goes to amazon.com — mark it as Safe.
Reveal the URL for 'Claim 90% Discount NOW'.
The URL goes to deals-4u-cheap.xyz with http:// — mark it Dangerous.

drDigitalSuccess: You spotted the phishing links! Always check where a link goes before clicking.
drDigitalHint: Click 'Reveal URL' on each link to see its real URL, then mark it Safe or Dangerous.
